lines.english: Dhanaasaree, The Word Of Devotee Trilochan Jee:
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਕ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਸੁਕ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਥਾਰੋ ਕਰਮੁ ਰੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: dukrit sukrit thaaro karam ree |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Pain and pleasure are the result of your own actions. ||1||Pause||
Why do you slander the Lord? You are ignorant and deluded.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਲ ਜਨ ਮਧੇ ਮਿਲੵਿੋ ਸਾਰਗ ਪਾਨ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: kul jan madhe milayio saarag paan re |
lines.english: Among the men of the moon's family, Krishna was born;

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਕਰਾ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਬਸਤਾ ਸੁਰਸਰੀ ਇਸਨਾਨ ਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: sankaraa masatak basataa surasaree isanaan re |
lines.english: The moon dwells in Shiva's forehead; it takes its cleansing bath in the Ganges.
even so, the stains from its past actions remain on the moon's face. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਸ੍ਵ ਕਾ ਦੀਪਕੁ ਸ੍ਵਾਮੀ ਤਾ ਚੇ ਰੇ ਸੁਆਰਥੀ ਪੰਖੀ ਰਾਇ ਗਰੁੜ ਤਾ ਚੇ ਬਾਧਵਾ ॥
lines.translit: bisv kaa deepak svaamee taa che re suaarathee pankhee raae garurr taa che baadhavaa |
lines.english: Aruna was a charioteer; his master was the sun, the lamp of the world. His brother was Garuda, the king of birds;
and yet, Aruna was made a cripple, because of the karma of his past actions. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਿਕ ਪਾਤਿਕ ਹਰਤਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਣ ਨਾਥੁ ਰੀ ਤੀਰਥਿ ਤੀਰਥਿ ਭ੍ਰਮਤਾ ਲਹੈ ਨ ਪਾਰੁ ਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: anik paatik harataa tribhavan naath ree teerath teerath bhramataa lahai na paar ree |
lines.english: Shiva, the destroyer of countless sins, the Lord and Master of the three worlds, wandered from sacred shrine to sacred shrine; he never found an end to them.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਸਸੀਅ ਧੇਨ ਲਛਿਮੀ ਕਲਪਤਰ ਸਿਖਰਿ ਸੁਨਾਗਰ ਨਦੀ ਚੇ ਨਾਥੰ ॥
lines.translit: amrit saseea dhen lachhimee kalapatar sikhar sunaagar nadee che naathan |
lines.english: Through the nectar, the moon, the wish-fulfilling cow, Lakshmi, the miraculous tree of life, Sikhar the sun's horse, and Dhanavantar the wise physician - all arose from the ocean, the lord of rivers;
And yet, he could not erase the karma of cutting off Brahma's head. ||3||
and yet, because of its karma, its saltiness has not left it. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਾਧੀਲੇ ਲੰਕਾ ਗੜੁ ਉਪਾੜੀਲੇ ਰਾਵਣ ਬਣੁ ਸਲਿ ਬਿਸਲਿ ਆਣਿ ਤੋਖੀਲੇ ਹਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: daadheele lankaa garr upaarreele raavan ban sal bisal aan tokheele haree |
lines.english: Hanuman burnt the fortress of Sri Lanka, uprooted the garden of Raawan, and brought healing herbs for the wounds of Lachhman, pleasing Lord Raamaa;

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੂਰਬਲੋ ਕ੍ਰਿਤ ਕਰਮੁ ਨ ਮਿਟੈ ਰੀ ਘਰ ਗੇਹਣਿ ਤਾ ਚੇ ਮੋਹਿ ਜਾਪੀਅਲੇ ਰਾਮ ਚੇ ਨਾਮੰ ॥
lines.translit: poorabalo krit karam na mittai ree ghar gehan taa che mohi jaapeeale raam che naaman |
lines.english: The karma of past actions cannot be erased, O wife of my house; this is why I chant the Name of the Lord.
and yet, because of his karma, he could not be rid of his loin cloth. ||5||
Sri Sain:
So prays Trilochan, Dear Lord. ||6||1||
I am a sacrifice to the Lord of Lakshmi. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਤ ਮੰਗਲੁ ਰਾਜਾ ਰਾਮ ਰਾਇ ਕੋ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: nit mangal raajaa raam raae ko |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Again and again, hail to You, Lord King, Ruler of all! ||1||Pause||
With incense, lamps and ghee, I offer this lamp-lit worship service.
Hail to You, Lord, hail to You!
Sublime is the lamp, and pure is the wick.
You are immaculate and pure, O Brilliant Lord of Wealth! ||2||
Raamaanand knows the devotional worship of the Lord.
The Lord of the world, of wondrous form, has carried me across the terrifying world-ocean.
Says Sain, remember the Lord, the embodiment of supreme joy! ||4||2||
He says that the Lord is all-pervading, the embodiment of supreme joy. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਯਉ ਦੇਵਾ ਕਾਇਅਉ ਦੇਵਲ ਕਾਇਅਉ ਜੰਗਮ ਜਾਤੀ ॥
lines.translit: kaayau devaa kaaeaau deval kaaeaau jangam jaatee |
lines.english: Within the body, the Divine Lord is embodied. The body is the temple, the place of pilgrimage, and the pilgrim.
Peepaa:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਇਅਉ ਧੂਪ ਦੀਪ ਨਈਬੇਦਾ ਕਾਇਅਉ ਪੂਜਉ ਪਾਤੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: kaaeaau dhoop deep neebedaa kaaeaau poojau paatee |1|
lines.english: Within the body are incense, lamps and offerings. Within the body are the flower offerings. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਇਆ ਬਹੁ ਖੰਡ ਖੋਜਤੇ ਨਵ ਨਿਧਿ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: kaaeaa bahu khandd khojate nav nidh paaee |
lines.english: I searched throughout many realms, but I found the nine treasures within the body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਕਛੁ ਆਇਬੋ ਨਾ ਕਛੁ ਜਾਇਬੋ ਰਾਮ ਕੀ ਦੁਹਾਈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: naa kachh aaeibo naa kachh jaaeibo raam kee duhaaee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Nothing comes, and nothing goes; I pray to the Lord for Mercy. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੀਪਾ ਪ੍ਰਣਵੈ ਪਰਮ ਤਤੁ ਹੈ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਹੋਇ ਲਖਾਵੈ ॥੨॥੩॥
lines.translit: peepaa pranavai param tat hai satigur hoe lakhaavai |2|3|
lines.english: Peepaa prays, the Lord is the supreme essence; He reveals Himself through the True Guru. ||2||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡੇ ਸੋਈ ਪਿੰਡੇ ਜੋ ਖੋਜੈ ਸੋ ਪਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: jo brahamandde soee pindde jo khojai so paavai |
lines.english: The One who pervades the Universe also dwells in the body; whoever seeks Him, finds Him there.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਜਨ ਤੁਮਰੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰੰਤੇ ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਕਾਜ ਸਵਾਰਤਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jo jan tumaree bhagat karante tin ke kaaj savaarataa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: You are the Arranger of the affairs of those humble beings who perform Your devotional worship service. ||1||Pause||
O Lord of the world, this is Your lamp-lit worship service.
Dhannaa:
My mind shall ever be pleased.
Lentils, flour and ghee - these things, I beg of You.
And grain of seven kinds - I beg of You. ||1||
A milk cow, and a water buffalo, I beg of You,
Shoes, fine clothes,
and a fine Turkestani horse.
- Your humble servant Dhanna begs for these things, Lord. ||2||4||
A good wife to care for my home
